title: Signature of spin liquid state in a frustrated 3D antiferromagnet
authors: Satish Kumar, U. Jena, A. Bandhopadhay, G. B. G. Stenning, D. T. Adroja, S. Petit, P. Khuntia
abstract: Frustrated pyrochlore lattices in transition-metal oxides provide an ideal platform for realizing exotic quantum states, including spin liquids with unconventional low-energy excitations arising from the macroscopic ground-state degeneracy of corner-sharing tetrahedral networks. Here, we report the synthesis and comprehensive characterization of ZnCrGaO$4$, a frustrated three-dimensional pyrochlore-like magnet in which intrinsic cation ordering gives rise to unavoidable atomic-site disorder. A Curie--Weiss analysis of the high-temperature magnetic susceptibility yields a large negative Curie--Weiss temperature, $θ{\mathrm{CW}} \approx -205$ K, indicating dominant antiferromagnetic exchange interactions ($J/k_{\mathrm{B}} \sim 55$ K) between Cr$^{3+}$ ($S = 3/2$) moments. Despite the presence of strong antiferromagnetic interactions, no signature of long-range magnetic ordering is observed down to 125 mK, as evidenced by specific-heat and ac-susceptibility measurements. Furthermore, the absence of bifurcation between zero-field-cooled and field-cooled dc magnetic susceptibilities measured at 0.01 T indicates the absence of spin freezing, which is further supported by the frequency-independent ac susceptibility down to 250 mK. The presence of broad maxima in the magnetic specific heat and ac susceptibility at low temperatures suggests the development of short-range spin correlations within a dynamic magnetic state. In addition, the low-temperature specific heat follows a power-law behavior below 1 K, indicating the presence of unconventional low-energy excitations and algebraic spin correlations. These results provide compelling evidence for a dynamic correlated ground state in ZnCrGaO$_4$, establishing it as a promising platform for exploring highly frustrated $S > 1/2$ three-dimensional quantum magnets and potential spin-liquid behavior.
summary: This paper reports the discovery of a dynamic, correlated magnetic state in the highly frustrated 3D magnet ZnCrGaO4. By utilizing intrinsic disorder to suppress long-range ordering, the material exhibits signatures of a quantum spin liquid, including power-law specific heat. This makes it a significant platform for studying 3D quantum magnetism and disorder-driven criticality.
main_problem: The study aims to determine the magnetic ground state of the highly frustrated 3D antiferromagnet ZnCrGaO4 and investigate whether intrinsic cation disorder can stabilize a quantum spin liquid state.
main_result: The researchers found no evidence of long-range magnetic ordering or spin freezing down to 125 mK, instead observing signatures of a dynamic, correlated ground state with power-law specific heat.
method: The study uses a combination of solid-state synthesis, powder X-ray diffraction, and low-temperature thermodynamic measurements including AC/DC magnetic susceptibility and specific heat.
model_or_system: The system is ZnCrGaO4 (ZCGO), a 3D pyrochlore-like magnet with a cubic spinel structure where Cr3+ (S=3/2) ions form a frustrated network of corner-sharing tetrahedra subject to intrinsic site disorder from Ga3+ substitution.
key_observables: Curie-Weiss temperature, magnetic susceptibility (ZFC/FC and AC), magnetic specific heat, magnetization, and magnetic entropy.
important_parameters: Curie-Weiss temperature (theta_CW approx -205 K), frustration parameter (f approx 1640), exchange interaction (J/kB approx 55 K), and temperature scales down to 125 mK.
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes the validity of the Curie-Weiss law at high temperatures and uses a mean-field approximation to estimate exchange strength.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows Rietveld refinement and the tetrahedral network with disorder; Figure 2 shows ZFC/FC magnetic susceptibility; Figure 4 shows a phase diagram of the ZnCr(2-x)Ga(x)O4 compositional range.
paper_structure: The paper begins with structural characterization, moves to high-temperature magnetic properties, investigates low-temperature susceptibility to rule out spin freezing, analyzes specific heat for evidence of excitations, and concludes with a discussion on the stabilized spin-liquid state.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. (a) The Rietveld refinement profile of the powder X- ray diffraction pattern taken at room temperature. The inset depicts a 3D network of corner-sharing tetrahedra formed by Cr3+ ions, with unavoidable 50% site sharing of Cr3+ with Ga3+ ions. The inset also illustrates an aperiodic distribu- tion of hexagonal spin loops in ZnCrGaO4. Unlike the peri- odic hexagon configurations found in pure ZnCr2O4, the un- avoidable site sharing of nonmagnetic Ga3+ ions at Cr3+ ion site destroys long-range structural periodicity, leaving a dis- ordered array of intact, local hexagonal clusters (color-coded to indicate localized loop orientations).

FIG. 2. (a) Zero-field-cooled and field-cooled magnetic susceptibility as a function of temperature under an applied field of 0.01 T. The inset displays the M vs H in five quadrants measured at 1.9 K. (b) Temperature dependence of the magnetic susceptibility (χ) (Left, Y-Axis) and inverse susceptibility (χ) (Right, Y-Axis) measured at an applied field of 1 T. The solid red line represents a Curie–Weiss fit to the high-temperature data. (c) Magnetization as a function of external magnetic field at several temperatures.

FIG. 3. (a) The temperature dependence of the magnetic specific heat (Cm) after subtracting the lattice part from the total specific heat. The solid lines depicts a fit to Cm ∼T 2. Full details of lattice subtraction and DE-fit are given in Supplementary Material [37]. (b) The magnetic entropy changes ∆Sm, which is around 37% of the expected value. i.e. Rln(4) for one Cr3+

FIG. 4. Magnetic phase diagram of frustrated pyrochlore, ZnCr(2x)Ga(2−2x)O4 depicting exotic phases. The phase dia- gram is determined by M¨ossbauer spectra [solid-circle ●], neu- tron diffraction [solid-triangle ▲], and magnetic susceptibility (χ) [solid-square ■]. A spin-glass phase has been observed in the region 0.6≤x ≤0.85, highlighted in yellow [54–56]. The star-line (-✫-) represents the behavior of the θCW at the right y-axis depending upon the chemical pressure (x). The inset shows a typical shape of rugged free-energy E(z) landscapes for spin glass ground state, where z represents the different spin configuration [57].
